pbcoreDescriptionDocument.pbcoreTitle: Cases Of Whooping Cough On The Rise In Ottawa County
pbcoreDescriptionDocument.pbcoreDescription:
So far this year, county health spokesperson Shannon Felgner says they have 14 cases reported. In 2007 and 2008 the number of cases each year never exceeded the single digits. Symptoms include sneezing, runny nose and a fever - what make it distinct is the whooping type cough.

'It's a very strong cough and often times people make a whoop when they're trying to catch their breath because they're coughing so hard. Sometimes when children get whooping cough they have bouts of coughing so hard that it ends up causing nausea and vomiting.'

Felgner notes the effectiveness of the vaccine wanes over time so a booster shot is required for adults.

'While it may not have long term effects for you or your healthy child it could be very severe and could have grave consequences for a young infant.'

A new booster shot that's came out a couple years ago includes vaccines for both tetanus and pertussis.
